title: dalbergia ovata
intro: Dalbergia ovata, also known as the "Indian Rosewood" or "Palisandrin", is a plant species in the Fabaceae family, native to the Indian subcontinent. The medicinal properties of Dalbergia ovata include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides. The health benefits of using Dalbergia ovata include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Dalbergia ovata include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be prepared by boiling the bark, leaves, or roots in water and then filtering or mixing with other herbs for various medicinal uses.
